team-maker-codebase-analysis.content:
## Team Maker Codebase Analysis (2026-03-26)

### Project Overview
Web-based manager for running multiple Claude Code CLI instances from a browser. Spawns PTY-backed Claude Code processes and streams terminal I/O over WebSocket to an xterm.js frontend. Supports multi-agent orchestration with task boards, messaging, and shared context.

### Tech Stack
- Runtime: Node.js with ES Modules
- Server: Express 5.1 + ws 8.18 + node-pty 0.10.x
- MCP: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk 1.27.1 with Zod schemas
- Frontend: Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS, xterm.js 5.5.0, marked, DOMPurify
- Theme: Catppuccin Mocha
- Persistence: JSON file at ~/.team-maker/state.json

### Server Modules (11 total)
1. index.js — Express HTTP + WebSocket server, all REST endpoints, event broadcasting, shutdown
2. sessionManager.js — Session + SessionManager, PTY lifecycle, JSONL watcher, question detection, idle management
3. teamManager.js — Team CRUD, agent spawning, MCP config generation, model routing
4. taskBoard.js — Task states (pending→assigned→in_progress→completed|failed), dependencies, complexity
5. messageQueue.js — Inter-agent messaging (queue + PTY injection), persistence
6. contextStore.js — Team-scoped key-value knowledge store (500KB cap, LRU eviction)
7. stateStore.js — JSON persistence with dot-path ops, debounced writes
8. jsonlParser.js — Claude Code JSONL log parser, fs.watch + adaptive polling
9. promptBuilder.js — Orchestrator prompt generation, built-in/extra role definitions
10. projectMemoryStore.js — File-based project memory (<cwd>/.team-maker/project-memory.json)
11. templateStore.js — Team role template CRUD

### Frontend Files
- public/index.html — Main HTML with sidebar, tabs, modals, CDN deps
- public/css/style.css — Catppuccin Mocha themed styles
- public/js/app.js — Application logic (Maps-based state, WebSocket, xterm.js)
- public/usage.html — Standalone usage page

### REST API: ~45+ endpoints across sessions, teams, agents, messages, tasks, context, project memory, events, files, templates
### WebSocket: bidirectional (attach/resize/input + terminal data/events/broadcasts)
### MCP Tools: 17 tools (spawn_agent, list_agents, send_message, check_inbox, mark_read, create_task, claim_task, complete_task, fail_task, get_tasks, store_context, query_context, list_context, store_project_memory, query_project_memory, list_project_memory, deprecate_project_memory)
team-maker-codebase-analysis.summary: Full codebase analysis: 11 server modules, vanilla frontend, 45+ REST endpoints, 17 MCP tools, WebSocket protocol

sdd-git-diff-toggle.content:
# SDD Cycle Complete — Git Diff Toggle Feature

**Status**: [✓] Validated  
**Date**: 2026-03-26  
**Validator**: Agent 3 (5b76ea97-007e-4026-9c99-53ff0e2c6534)  
**Scribe**: Agent 4 (d0593a10-0a27-4069-95a7-65d8d1205a23)

## Summary

The git-diff-toggle feature has completed the full SDD cycle: spec written, approved, built, validated, and documented. All 14 acceptance criteria passed validation.

## What Was Built

### Backend (`server/index.js`)
- **Endpoint**: `GET /api/git-diff?file=<path>&cwd=<dir>`
- Returns: `{ diff: string, hasDiff: boolean }`
- Runs `git diff HEAD -- <file>` with security checks (symlink resolution + traversal validation)
- Error handling: 400 (missing params), 403 (path security), 404 (file not found), 500 (git unavailable)

### Frontend (`public/js/app.js`, `public/css/style.css`)
- **"View Diff" toggle** in file viewer header (appears only when `hasDiff: true`)
- **Format selector** ("Unified" | "Split") visible only when toggle is On
- **Unified diff**: green adds, red removes, blue hunk headers; line numbers in gutter
- **Split diff**: Before/After columns side-by-side
- All elements use Catppuccin Mocha theme colors
- Diff fetched concurrently with file read; toggling/format-switching uses cached diff (no re-fetch)

## Spec Files Updated

- **docs/specs/02-contracts.md** (lines 733-755): `GET /api/git-diff` endpoint marked `[✓] Validated`
- **docs/specs/04-frontend.md** (lines 703-784): `Files Panel — Git Diff Toggle` component marked `[✓] Validated`

## Validation Results

✅ **All 14 acceptance criteria PASSED**:

Backend (6 criteria):
- [x] `GET /api/git-diff` endpoint exists with correct path/params
- [x] Returns `{ diff: string, hasDiff: boolean }` shape
- [x] Path security checks (symlink + traversal)
- [x] 400 for missing params
- [x] 500 for non-git repo / git unavailable
- [x] Empty diff for untracked/unchanged files

Frontend (8 criteria):
- [x] Concurrent diff fetch on file open
- [x] Toggle shown only when `hasDiff: true`
- [x] No toggle when `hasDiff: false`
- [x] Toggle defaults to Off
- [x] Toggle On/Off switches view correctly (no re-fetch)
- [x] Mauve accent on Toggle On state
- [x] Format selector visible only when toggle is On
- [x] Unified/Split rendering with correct colors

## Documentation Status

- **Spec headers**: File-level status remains `[x] Done` (other components in those files not yet validated)
- **README.md**: No changes needed (shows file-level status only; component-level status reflected in spec files themselves)
- **CLAUDE.md**: No changes needed (endpoint documented in spec as intended; no architectural changes)

SDD cycle closed and documented.
sdd-git-diff-toggle.summary: Git diff toggle feature fully validated and documented. All 14 acceptance criteria pass.

sdd-team-pause-resume.content:
# Team Pause/Resume Feature — SDD Closure Summary
**Date**: 2026-03-27
**Validation**: 28/28 criteria PASS

## What Was Built

A team pause/resume capability that suspends background monitoring timers without killing PTY processes, then restores them on resume. An auto-pause fires automatically when all tasks on the board settle to terminal state.

## Files Changed

### server/sessionManager.js
- Added `suspendMonitoring()`: clears `_healthCheckInterval` and `_idleCheckTimer`; PTY stays alive; session status unchanged; idempotent (null-checks before clear)
- Added `resumeMonitoring()`: guards on `this.status === "running"`; calls `_startHealthCheck()` and `_startIdleCheck()` to restart fresh timers

### server/teamManager.js
- Added `pause(teamId)`: returns null if team not found or status !== "running"; sets `team.status = "paused"`; calls `suspendMonitoring()` on each live session; persists via `_persistTeam()`
- Added `resume(teamId)`: returns null if team not found or status !== "paused"; sets `team.status = "running"`; calls `resumeMonitoring()` on sessions with status "running"; persists
- Team status values: `"running"` | `"paused"` | `"stopped"`

### server/taskBoard.js
- Extended EventEmitter
- Added `_checkAllSettled(teamId)` called after `completeTask()` and `failTask()`
- Emits `{ type: "all-tasks-settled", teamId }` when `summary.pending === 0 && summary.assigned === 0 && summary.in_progress === 0 && summary.total > 0`

### server/index.js
- `POST /api/teams/:teamId/pause` — calls `teamManager.pause()`; 400 on null; broadcasts `{ type: "team-update", event: "team-paused", teamId, source: "manual" }`
- `POST /api/teams/:teamId/resume` — calls `teamManager.resume()`; 400 on null; broadcasts `{ type: "team-update", event: "team-resumed", teamId }`
- Subscribes to `taskBoard` `all-tasks-settled` event → calls `pause()` → broadcasts with `source: "auto"`

### public/js/app.js
- `pauseTeam(teamId)` / `resumeTeam(teamId)` functions calling respective REST endpoints
- `renderTeamItem()` updated: Pause (⏸) for "running", Resume (▶) for "paused", Relaunch (↺) for "stopped"
- "paused" badge: Catppuccin Yellow `#f9e2af`
- `handleTeamUpdate()` handles `team-paused` and `team-resumed` WS events (no page reload)
- Auto-pause toast: shown only when `source === "auto"`
- New Agent button disabled for paused teams
- Empty state for paused active team: "Team \"{name}\" is paused — click ▶ to resume"
- Usage auto-refresh stops on `team-paused`, restarts on `team-resumed`
- Idle warning/killed overlays suppressed for sessions in paused teams

### public/css/style.css
- `.team-item-badge.paused { color: #f9e2af }` (Catppuccin Yellow)

### public/index.html
- No structural changes needed (buttons rendered dynamically)

## Key Design Decisions

1. **PTY survives pause** — timers are cleared but PTY process is not killed; sessions remain interactive
2. **On server restart, paused → stopped** — `restoreFromState()` always sets status to "stopped"; intentional (PTYs don't survive restarts)
3. **Auto-pause requires total > 0** — prevents auto-pause on empty boards
4. **MCP tools unaffected** — mcpServer.js unchanged; all 17 tools remain functional while paused
5. **Resume only restarts monitoring for "running" sessions** — sessions in other states are left alone

## Spec Files Updated
- `docs/specs/02-contracts.md`: pause/resume endpoints → `[✓] Validated`; Last Updated: 2026-03-27
- `docs/specs/03-backend.md`: Team Pause/Resume component → `[✓] Validated`; Last Updated: 2026-03-27
- `docs/specs/04-frontend.md`: Team Pause/Resume UI component → `[✓] Validated`; Last Updated: 2026-03-27

## CLAUDE.md Updates
- `sessionManager.js` description: added `suspendMonitoring()`/`resumeMonitoring()`
- `teamManager.js` description: added `pause()`/`resume()` and status values
- `taskBoard.js` description: added EventEmitter extension and `all-tasks-settled` event
sdd-team-pause-resume.summary: Team pause/resume feature fully validated. 28/28 criteria pass. Adds paused status, suspendMonitoring/resumeMonitoring, auto-pause on task settlement, REST endpoints, frontend UI.
